camera.notes: Orthographic package smoke camera over the cooked 2D scene.

inputMap.purpose: Move the player sprite through deterministic 2D gameplay smoke rows.
Exercise audio, contact, and animation feedback in the package proof.

coreLoop.objective: Move the cooked player sprite through the packaged scene while package-visible systems report deterministic gameplay counters.
coreLoop.feedback: Sprite animation, HUD submission, runtime UI workbench rows, audio cue, physics contacts, navigation, AI, and simulation counters are emitted by selected package recipes.
coreLoop.winState: Installed package recipes report clean 2D package, gameplay systems, runtime UI workbench, native sprite, and simulation evidence.
coreLoop.failState: Missing package files, invalid scene references, or unsupported broad claims fail schema/static/package validation.
coreLoop.restart: Rerun the package smoke from the manifest-derived runtime package files without mutating cooked payloads.

sceneList.purpose: Desktop runtime configuration shipped through runtimePackageFiles.
Package index used by installed validation and runtime scene loading.

assetRequests.purpose: Reviewed RGBA8 source frames feed first-party sprite atlas authoring before cooked runtime consumption.
First-party tilemap metadata drives deterministic visible tile counters.
Reviewed sandbox-world tile definitions, palette brushes, chunk templates, procedural seeds, and package-update rows feed deterministic cook review before runtime package mutation.
Cooked UI atlas metadata proves image and glyph atlas palette handoff counters before renderer submission.
Cooked audio cue proves device-independent audio feedback.

qualityGates.evidence: Installed package validation loads manifest-derived config, package index, and cooked scene payloads.
Gameplay systems, native sprite, UI, audio, physics, navigation, and AI counters are selected package evidence.
RPG systems smoke reports deterministic stat, progression, skill, equipment, combat-loop intent, reward, save-validation, replay-hash, and zero side-effect counters.

Sandbox world smoke reports deterministic chunk, resident chunk, existing cell, placement/destruction intent, cost, mutation, persistence, repairable persistence, unsafe rejection, replay-hash, and zero world/persistence/package side-effect counters.

Sandbox package budget smoke reports sandbox_package_budget_status=ready, deterministic row/byte/chunk/dirty/draw/light/persistence/streaming counters, positive replay hash, and zero package IO, native handle, arbitrary shell, external download, source image decode, broad multiplayer, backend submission, native texture ownership, renderer/RHI residency, and broad renderer quality side-effect counters.

Performance baseline smoke reports deterministic performance_baseline_* frame/profile samples over the selected D3D12 package lane, including fixed warm-up, sample-frame count, counter/profile names, p95/p99 frame-time budget rows, and zero diagnostics.

Long-run readiness smoke reports deterministic long_run_readiness_* frame, memory, diagnostics, clean-shutdown, and zero unsupported side-effect counters over the selected D3D12 package lane without claiming broad optimization.

Host-owned long-run readiness soak is an opt-in 108000-frame D3D12 package lane validation recipe. It is excluded from default validation, requires explicit host acknowledgement, and must not promote broad long-running readiness or vendor/OS tuning claims.

Sandbox authoring review smoke reports sandbox_authoring_review_status=ready, deterministic tile definition, palette brush, chunk template, procedural seed, changed-file, dependency-edge, and preview-hash counters, with sandbox_authoring_review_external_image_decoding_invoked=0, sandbox_authoring_review_external_download_invoked=0, sandbox_authoring_review_importer_plugin_invoked=0, sandbox_authoring_review_package_apply_invoked=0, and zero diagnostic counters.

Simulation management smoke reports deterministic resource balance, job assignment, logistics transfer, economy summary, population need, schedule, save-review, dashboard, replay-hash, and zero side-effect counters.
2D gameplay execution loop smoke reports a ready first-party scheduler/world/interaction composition with deterministic step, row, replay-hash, diagnostic, and zero side-effect counters.

2D sprite atlas residency smoke reports ready cooked-package atlas page residency, one positive page row, one upload-handoff row, four resident bytes, clean diagnostics, no runtime source decode, no renderer/RHI residency ownership, no public native handles, and no renderer upload invocation.

2D sprite throughput smoke reports ready dense 512, dense 4096, and 12000-logical projectile workload rows with deterministic draw, instance, upload, atlas-page, material-lane, culling, retained timing artifact, clean diagnostic, and zero unsupported broad/backend/Metal claim counters.

2D physics runtime extension smoke reports selected exact sweep/TOI, no-hit, initial-overlap, kinematic contact, distance/hinge/prismatic/spring joint, trigger enter/exit, clean diagnostic, zero native-handle, zero middleware, and zero dynamic-vs-dynamic CCD claim counters.

2D input device production UX smoke reports deterministic gesture/action, device assignment, per-device profile, symbolic glyph lookup, keyboard layout label, clean diagnostic, zero native-handle, zero UI rendering, zero input middleware, zero glyph rendering, and zero UI widget counters.

Network replication smoke reports network_replication_status=host_evidence_required, network_replication_reviewed=1, network_replication_ready=0, network_replication_object_rows=2, network_replication_input_rows=2, network_replication_snapshot_rows=2, network_replication_rollback_rows=1, network_replication_transport_host_evidence=0, network_replication_diagnostics=0, positive replay-hash, and zero network/world/rollback side-effect counters.

Network production security smoke reports deterministic network_production_security_* fields for a reviewed threat model, session lifecycle, connection state, channel policy, sequence/replay, input, snapshot, and rollback-window evidence rows, host-gated loopback/replication readiness, zero external network/thread/save/world side effects, clean unsupported-online-claim rows, and a positive replay hash without claiming matchmaking, NAT traversal, encryption/authentication, cloud backend, or broad online readiness.
Runtime menu HUD smoke reports deterministic display, command, dialogue, and input-binding prompt counters.

Runtime UI standard widgets smoke reports runtime_ui_standard_widgets_ready=1, first-party HP/MP/SP meter rows, pause/settings menu actions, official-documentation provenance rows, first-party design rows, accessibility/localization/style-token counters, and zero external engine code, asset, or UI middleware counters.

Runtime UI binding smoke reports runtime_ui_binding_ready=1, typed binding rows, command availability rows, focus scopes, navigation edges, input routing readiness, and zero gameplay command execution counters.

Runtime UI workbench smoke reports deterministic menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard table and graph, focus navigation, text input, localization identity, and accessibility identity counters.

Runtime UI production stack smoke reports side-effect-free shaping direction/script/language rows, glyph ids/clusters/advances/offsets/fallback/bidi/line-break evidence, glyph bitmap/metrics/pixel-format rows, selected package counters, host gates, zero dependency-gated/skipped/adapter-invoked/unsupported rows, and a positive replay hash without claiming broad production UI readiness.

Runtime UI TSF native IME session smoke reports selected Windows TSF thread/document/context evidence with runtime_ui_tsf_session_ready=1, runtime_ui_tsf_composition_rows=3, runtime_ui_tsf_candidate_intent_rows=1, runtime_ui_tsf_text_area_rows=1, runtime_ui_ime_native_candidate_ui_ready=0, runtime_ui_ime_cross_platform_ready=0, and runtime_ui_tsf_diagnostics=0 without exposing TSF, COM, Win32, or native handles.

Runtime UI UIA accessibility publication smoke reports selected Windows UI Automation provider evidence with runtime_ui_uia_provider_ready=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_nodes=2, runtime_ui_accessibility_action_rows=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_event_rows=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_cross_platform_ready=0, runtime_ui_accessibility_native_handles_exposed=0, and runtime_ui_uia_diagnostics=0 without exposing UIA, COM, HWND, Win32, or native handles.

Runtime UI renderer atlas handoff smoke loads cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 metadata from runtimePackageFiles, builds image and glyph palettes, plans UiRendererSubmission execution rows, submits one glyph and one image through MK_ui_renderer, then reports selected package counters for renderer layers, batches, clip rects, unresolved resources, and zero native handles without source image decoding, live glyph atlas generation, renderer upload APIs, native handles, or broad UI rendering claims.

Runtime UI platform package smoke runs --require-runtime-ui-platform-package and reports runtime_ui_platform_runtime_package_ready=1, runtime_ui_platform_production_ready=0 until the wrapper supplies visible editor shell evidence, runtime_ui_platform_clean_room_ready=1, selected DirectWrite/TSF/UIA/D3D12 adapter counters, zero external engine parity claims, and zero public native handles.
Audio gameplay mixer smoke reads the cooked package audio payload, then reports deterministic bus, cue, trigger, command, pause, fade, loop, spatial, render, and sample diagnostic counters.

Audio production smoke reports audio_production_* fields for reviewed decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, DSP, spatial/listener, HRTF host-gate, WASAPI device lifecycle, unsupported-claim, side-effect, diagnostic, and replay-hash evidence. It remains host-gated for device and HRTF proof and does not claim broad codec support, middleware parity, HRTF execution parity, native handles, background streaming execution, or general production audio quality.

Source image/audio codec review smoke reports source_image_audio_codec_review_status=ready, six reviewed rows, exact vcpkg.libspng and vcpkg.miniaudio dependency/legal records, PNG RGBA8 pixel format evidence, audio sample-format evidence, selected package evidence, zero unsupported claims, zero SVG/background/HRTF/runtime-source/native/package side effects, and a positive replay hash without broad image or audio codec readiness.

Source Pulse package smoke reports 2d_source_pulse_status=ready, three reviewed file-watch change rows, native backend and polling fallback evidence rows, one reviewed runtime replacement safe-point commit, runtime scene validation and operator safe-point review gates, zero editor-core/arbitrary-shell/package-script/native-handle side effects, and zero external engine schema, asset, or code use.
Simulation orchestration smoke reports fixed-step command playback and clean diagnostics without running broad game rules.

aiOwnedSourceRoots.evidence: Generated game-owned files are confined to this root; shared repository surfaces require a separate developer-owned task.
Runtime package payload changes must stay manifest-listed and validation-recipe-backed.
Generated source authoring fixtures remain game-local before reviewed cook/package handoff.
Generated shader source fixtures remain game-local and require shader-toolchain validation before package claims.

forbiddenSharedPaths.reason: Engine internals require a separate developer-owned capability task.
Editor internals are outside generated-game mutation scope.
Shared automation changes require a repository workflow task.
Shared schema changes require explicit agent-surface synchronization.
Machine-readable engine contracts are composed from reviewed fragments only.
CI workflow changes are outside generated-game mutation scope.
Plan and production-ledger changes are developer-owned repository work.
Shared game registration is changed only by reviewed scaffolding tools.

remediationActions.trigger: A manifest-declared runtime package payload is missing or not registered.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Repair only game-local runtime payload registration before rerunning package validation.

remediationActions.trigger: A requested change targets a forbidden shared repository path.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Stop and record a developer-owned handoff instead of editing shared surfaces.

remediationActions.trigger: A requested game behavior needs an unsupported reusable engine capability.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Persist a reviewed aiWorkflow.engineCapabilityHandoffs row with first-party public-contract evidence.

remediationActions.trigger: A selected validation recipe fails after generated game-local mutation.
remediationActions.expectedResult: Repair game-owned rows without weakening validation, deleting evidence, or changing shared tooling.

reviewedToolSurfaces.evidence: Plans deterministic first-party placeholder source documents and provenance rows without external asset downloads.
Routes placeholder source documents through registered source cook/package planning before runtime package consumption.

packageHandoff.evidence: Package handoff stays manifest-listed, first-party, and validation-recipe-backed before runtime use.

replacementWorkflow.evidence: Placeholder replacement must keep the stable asset key, regenerate or review a game-owned source document, preserve provenance/license rows, and reroute through reviewed cook/package handoff before runtime use.

packageProvenance.evidence: First-party source manifest rows map selected 2D source categories to manifest-listed cooked package outputs while keeping source files out of runtimePackageFiles.

cookingPolicy.evidence: The descriptor is a clean-break first-party source contract; runtime source parsing remains unsupported and package mutation stays behind reviewed cook/package handoff.

incrementalRecook.evidence: Incremental recook invalidation is driven only by content hashes and declared dependency graph rows, then stops at a reviewed package safe point.

packageBudget.evidence: Package budget rows fail closed when selected cooked outputs exceed per-class or aggregate byte budgets.

replay.evidence: Replay hashing covers selected deterministic cook outputs, content hashes, dependency graph rows, source provenance, license status, package budgets, and invalidation rows.

qualityGateRows.evidence: Selected production atlas rows record paddingPixels=2 and bleedPixels=1, and pack_production_sprite_atlas_rgba8 extrudes decoded RGBA8 edge pixels through padded rects.
Rotation is an explicit disabled policy and every selected placement records rotated=false.
Player atlas rows select require-power-of-two-pages with 1024x1024 pages; UI atlas rows record keep-tight-bounds.
The selected player texture cook output records two deterministic atlas pages.
Placements are manifest rows keyed by source manifest ids and produced by deterministic decoded RGBA8 packing order.
Selected atlas rows target rgba8_unorm only.
Selected atlas rows record base-level-only mip policy; generated mips remain outside this contract.
Atlas runtimePackageFile rows reference manifest runtimePackageFiles and deterministic cook output paths.

packageInclusion.evidence: Selected production atlas package inclusion is proved only through manifest runtimePackageFiles rows and deterministic cook output rows.

reviewedRecipeSurfaces.evidence: Plans allowlisted validation recipe argv and host gates without evaluating raw manifest command strings.
Executes only reviewed validation recipes while preserving host-gate decisions and recipe output summaries.
Reviews package smoke stdout, gameplay counters, and diagnostics before choosing mutation-ledger remediation.

failureClassifications.trigger: A selected recipe reports a missing manifest-listed runtimePackageFiles payload or package artifact.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair the game-owned package file or manifest registration through reviewed package surfaces.

failureClassifications.trigger: A manifest, package index, scene, asset, or recipe reference points at an undeclared or invalid row.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair game-owned descriptor rows without deleting validation evidence.

failureClassifications.trigger: The selected runtimeSceneValidationTargets row, scene asset key, package index, or package smoke scene binding is missing or invalid.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair reviewed scene/package descriptor rows without weakening the selected package playtest recipe.

failureClassifications.trigger: A recipe requires host gates that are unavailable or unacknowledged on the current machine.
failureClassifications.evidence: Record the host gate and do not bypass it or substitute an unrelated ready claim.

failureClassifications.trigger: A selected shader or package recipe reports missing D3D12, Vulkan, Metal, or shader toolchain evidence.
failureClassifications.evidence: Stop at a developer-owned toolchain or engine capability handoff when the game cannot repair the gap.

failureClassifications.trigger: Package stdout lacks expected gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI counters.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair game-owned rows or code through reviewed mutation surfaces without weakening expected counters.

failureClassifications.trigger: Runtime package load, index, asset payload, or runtime scene validation fails.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair reviewed runtime package files and package index rows before rerunning smoke recipes.

failureClassifications.trigger: The selected package playtest recipe cannot load the installed runtime package or package index.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair reviewed runtimePackageFiles and package artifacts before rerunning productized package playtest smoke.

failureClassifications.trigger: Reviewed recook-to-resident replacement evidence reports a recook failure or missing safe-point preflight.
failureClassifications.evidence: Stop at reviewed hot-reload safe-point remediation; do not run package scripts or infer active-session hot reload.

failureClassifications.trigger: Reviewed runtime replacement evidence reports a failed safe-point replacement or invalid resident catalog transition.
failureClassifications.evidence: Repair reviewed runtime package rows or developer-owned engine capability evidence without native handles.

failureClassifications.trigger: The selected package playtest requires a backend or runtime host that is unavailable on the current host.
failureClassifications.evidence: Record host-gated backend evidence and do not substitute another backend readiness claim.

failureClassifications.trigger: A remediation would touch shared repository paths, engine internals, raw shell, or unreviewed cooked packages.
failureClassifications.evidence: Stop at mutation-ledger remediation or developer-owned handoff instead of broadening game write scope.

remediationPolicy.evidence: All repair actions route through aiWorkflow.contentMutationLedger remediation rows and reviewed game-local command surfaces.

requiredEvidence: missing runtimePackageFiles row or package artifact path
stopConditions: target path leaves the game root

requiredEvidence: invalid manifest, package, scene, asset, or recipe reference
host gate id and unavailable host tool or device
stopConditions: an unrelated ready claim would replace host evidence

requiredEvidence: missing shader toolchain or backend evidence
stopConditions: engine internals or toolchain policy must change

requiredEvidence: expected gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI counter
package load, index, asset payload, or runtime scene validation diagnostic
stopConditions: package repair needs unreviewed cooked artifact mutation
runtime source parsing would be introduced

requiredEvidence: forbidden shared path or unsafe mutation request
stopConditions: shared repository files would be edited from the game lane
raw shell or native handles would be exposed

evidence: aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec.inputMap declares player actions and default bindings
selected package smokes report gameplay, renderer, UI, audio, physics, navigation, or AI feedback counters
validation remediation rows keep rerun-selected-validation-recipe policy
desktop runtime release target and selected installed package smoke are manifest-declared deterministic recipes
selected budget smoke reports package-visible entity scale, culling, draw, update, or frame budget evidence

unsupportedRows.reason: Rubric rows prove declared objective and deterministic evidence only.
No commercial-quality, balance, or content-market fitness claim is made.
Host-gated backend and platform evidence stays per recipe.
Budget rows are selected package evidence, not unbounded performance proof.
Only reviewed first-party/generated package content can satisfy rubric evidence.
The rubric does not tune or rebalance gameplay autonomously.
Rubric evidence uses manifest rows and first-party counters only.
Failing rubric rows route to remediation without weakening selected validation recipes.
This is a generated-game foundation rubric, not a broad production readiness claim.

gameplayContract.scene: GameEngine.Scene.v1 loaded from a cooked package and validated by mirakana::validate_playable_2d_scene

gameplayContract.physics: mirakana::PhysicsWorld2D deterministic contact and trigger counters
gameplayContract.renderer: mirakana::IRenderer from the desktop host with deterministic NullRenderer fallback or host-owned RHI-backed presentation when packaged shader artifacts are present
gameplayContract.spriteAnimationFlipbook: advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook_playback selects RuntimeSpriteFlipbookDirectionSetRow rows by gameplay state and direction, applies RuntimeSpriteFlipbookPlaybackMode loop/clamp policy, reports RuntimeSpriteFlipbookEventDesc rows, and emits sprite_flipbook_direction_sets, sprite_flipbook_event_rows, sprite_flipbook_playback_modes, sprite_flipbook_gameplay_state_rows, sprite_flipbook_events_sampled, and sprite_flipbook_playback_diagnostics through --require-sprite-animation
gameplayContract.productionTileRenderer: mirakana::plan_tile_chunk_renderer under --require-production-tile-renderer; selected smoke requires tile_chunk_renderer_status=ready, visible/opaque/transparent cells 3/2/1, sprite/draw rows 3/2, texture binds 1, tile_chunk_renderer_dirty_rebuild_cells=5, light rows 3, changed light cells 1, diagnostics 0, tile_chunk_renderer_native_texture_ownership_invoked=0, and zero backend submission/native texture ownership invocation without backend-specific tile submission, renderer/RHI residency, native handles, or broad renderer quality
gameplayContract.currentRuntime: host-gated Win32 desktop runtime package proof for 2D gameplay. D3D12 package window smoke uses packaged DXIL shader bytecode and --require-d3d12-shaders --require-d3d12-renderer through host-owned presentation; the native 2D sprite package proof adds --require-native-2d-sprites so cooked scene sprite texture/material identity, atlas-backed scene sprite planning counters (sprite_batch_plan_atlas_backed_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_sprites), selected sprite_batch_budget_* world/UI/effects budget profile counters, and HUD submission flow through the host-owned native RHI sprite overlay path with renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters. The sprite atlas source authoring target records reviewed RGBA8 frame rows plus selected page policy, pivot, and slice-border metadata for plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring, emits GameEngine.TextureSource.v1 plus GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 authoring files, and keeps those source files outside runtimePackageFiles before cooked runtime consumption. The sprite animation package proof adds a first-party cooked sprite_animation payload and --require-sprite-animation so deterministic flipbook ticks through advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook emit sprite_flipbook_frames_sampled and sprite_flipbook_frames_applied counters before sprite frame application emits sprite_animation_frames_sampled and sprite_animation_frames_applied counters. The tilemap runtime UX proof uses first-party GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata and --require-tilemap-runtime-ux so visible tile cells emit tilemap_cells_sampled and tilemap_diagnostics counters without claiming runtime image decoding, production atlas packing, or full tilemap editor UX. The production tile renderer proof uses mirakana::plan_tile_chunk_renderer and --require-production-tile-renderer so visible tile cells emit tile_chunk_renderer_status=ready, visible/opaque/transparent cells, sprite/draw rows, texture binds, dirty rebuild rows/cells, light rows, changed light cells, clean diagnostics, and zero backend submission/native texture ownership invocation without claiming backend-specific tile submission, renderer/RHI residency, native handles, or broad renderer quality. The gameplay systems package proof uses --require-gameplay-systems and --require-procedural-generation so 2D PhysicsWorld contacts/triggers, NavigationGrid path/agent movement, AI perception blackboard, behavior authoring validation, quest/dialogue state progression, quest/dialogue save-state validation, runtime inventory item pickup/state validation/recipe completion, reviewed construction placement validation plus runtime-scene placement intent planning, reviewed procedural generation plus procedural placement intent planning, behavior tree counters, and inputContextRebinding counters emit gameplay_systems_* plus input_context_rebinding_ready=1, input_rebinding_profile_overlays_applied=2, captured input_rebinding_action_capture_status/input_rebinding_axis_capture_status, input_rebinding_focus_retained=1, input_rebinding_presentation_rows=2, input_rebinding_glyph_lookup_keys=5, and input_rebinding_diagnostics=0 fields including gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_diagnostics, gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_trace_nodes, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_action_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_reward_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_state_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_final_workbench_quantity, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_validation_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_accepted_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_occupied_cells, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_object_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_encounter_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_loot_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_replay_hash, and gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_package_visible_rows. The runtime profile resume package proof uses --require-runtime-profile-resume so plan_runtime_session_profile_resume emits runtime_profile_resume_ready, loaded/defaulted document counters, and save/settings schema version evidence. The runtime menu HUD package proof uses --require-runtime-menu-hud so plan_runtime_menu_hud emits runtime_menu_hud_ready=1, runtime_menu_hud_display_rows=6, runtime_menu_hud_command_rows=2, runtime_menu_hud_dialogue_rows=1, and runtime_menu_hud_input_binding_prompt_rows=1. The audio gameplay mixer package proof uses --require-audio-gameplay-mixer over the cooked jump audio payload so plan_gameplay_audio_mix and AudioMixer emit audio_gameplay_mixer_ready=1, bus/cue/trigger/command, paused/faded bus, looping/spatial command, render, sample-sum, and sample diagnostic counters without native device handles. The audio production proof emits audio_production_* counters with audio_production_status=host_evidence_required, selected package evidence ready, device and HRTF host evidence unavailable, zero codec/background/middleware/HRTF/device-callback/device-IO side effects, two host-gate diagnostics, and a positive replay hash without broad codec support, middleware parity, HRTF execution parity, background streaming execution, native handles, or general production audio quality. The sprite effects particles package proof uses --require-sprite-effects-particles so plan_runtime_sprite_effect_particles emits deterministic sprite_effect_particles_* counters for spawn events, spawned/surviving/render rows, submitted rows, clean diagnostics, and budget diagnostic evidence without damage-number glyphs, GPU particles, scene mutation, renderer/RHI ownership, or native handles. The world-region streaming package proof uses --require-world-region-streaming so reviewed package candidates drive deterministic region load/keep/unload safe-point rows with budget evidence, missing-region diagnostics, reviewed package adoption counters, and clean safe-point diagnostics through world_region_streaming_* fields. The entity scale/culling package proof uses --require-entity-scale-culling so plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling emits deterministic package-visible entity_scale_culling_* counters for planned rows, visible/culled rows, LOD rows, update buckets, projected draw/update costs, budget-protected rows, clean diagnostics, and budget diagnostic evidence without renderer/RHI mutation. The 2D dense sprite throughput proof uses --require-2d-sprite-throughput so plan_sprite_batch_production_throughput plus plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling_sprite_draw_intents emit 2d_sprite_throughput_* counters for dense 512, dense 4096, and 12000-logical projectile workloads, 14 draw rows, 5376 instance rows, 319488 upload bytes, retained timing artifact hash 9817, clean diagnostics, and zero scene mutation, renderer ownership, native handle, broad optimization, cross-backend parity, or Metal readiness counters. The scripting sandbox policy proof uses --require-scripting-sandbox-policy so plan_runtime_script_sandbox emits deterministic scripting_sandbox_* counters for reviewed entrypoint rows, allowed permission rows, denied default-unsafe permission diagnostics, unsupported host API diagnostics, budget rows, replay seed evidence, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics without interpreting scripts, loading code, opening files, networking, spawning processes, binding native plugins, mutating packages, or exposing native handles. The networking foundation policy proof uses --require-networking-foundation-policy so plan_runtime_network_foundation emits deterministic networking_foundation_* counters for reviewed session rows, transport rows, replication channel rows, replay prerequisite rows, rejected unsafe transport requirements, insecure remote transport diagnostics, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics without opening sockets, adding transport middleware, performing encryption/authentication handshakes, matchmaking, rollback/prediction, NAT traversal, package mutation, or native handle exposure. The simulation orchestration package proof uses --require-simulation-orchestration so plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration emits deterministic simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics without running game rules, pacing renderer frames, implementing rollback/lockstep, mutating packages, or exposing native handles. The 2D gameplay execution loop package proof uses --require-2d-gameplay-execution-loop so execute_runtime_gameplay_loop_2d_step emits deterministic 2d_gameplay_execution_loop_* counters for a first-party scheduler/world/interaction composition with one fixed step, positive replay hash, clean diagnostics, and zero side effects without script VM execution, scene mutation, renderer/RHI ownership, package IO, native handles, or third-party engine API surface. The gameplay authoring review package proof uses --require-gameplay-authoring-review so review_gameplay_authoring_request emits deterministic gameplay_authoring_review_* counters for accepted feature rows, mutation ledger rows, remediation rows, unsupported capability/recipe/package evidence diagnostics, and unsupported broad-authoring claim diagnostics without applying fixes, mutating cooked packages, executing commands, or claiming autonomous commercial game design. The production authoring workflow proof uses --require-production-authoring-workflows so review_production_authoring_workflow emits deterministic production_authoring_workflow_* counters for six reviewed scene placement, quest/dialogue, item/economy, AI behavior, world region, and validation repair workflow rows, six mutation-ledger rows, and one validation-repair row that targets installed-2d-production-authoring-workflows-smoke while proving shared surface mutation, arbitrary shell, cooked package mutation, native/backend terms, and non-game target paths fail closed without file mutation, package IO, or command execution. The runtime UI workbench proof uses --require-runtime-ui-workbench so plan_runtime_ui_workbench emits runtime_ui_workbench_status=ready, runtime_ui_workbench_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_panels=5, runtime_ui_workbench_table_columns=3, runtime_ui_workbench_table_rows=2, runtime_ui_workbench_graph_series=2, runtime_ui_workbench_item_rows=3, runtime_ui_workbench_inventory_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_equipment_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_shop_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_text_inputs=1, runtime_ui_workbench_platform_text_input_requests=1, runtime_ui_workbench_focus_edges=4, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_refs=17, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_identity_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_refs=11, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_identity_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_renderer_submission=0, runtime_ui_workbench_text_shaping=0, runtime_ui_workbench_font_rasterization=0, runtime_ui_workbench_ime_sessions=0, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_bridge=0, runtime_ui_workbench_image_decoding=0, runtime_ui_workbench_native_platform=0, and runtime_ui_workbench_diagnostics=0 without renderer submission, text shaping, font rasterization, IME session execution, OS accessibility publication, image decoding, native platform work, UI middleware, or native handles. Vulkan package window smoke uses toolchain/host-gated SPIR-V shader bytecode and --require-vulkan-shaders --require-vulkan-renderer. public native or RHI handle access remains unsupported, real platform input glyph assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, multiplayer device assignment, native input handles, removed SDL3 input APIs, full runtime/game rebinding panels, broad production sprite batching readiness remains unsupported, broad background package streaming remains unsupported, broad entity scale execution/performance/GPU culling remains unsupported, broad scripting/mod runtime execution remains unsupported, broad multiplayer/networking readiness remains unsupported, broad deterministic multiplayer readiness remains unsupported, broad production runtime UI readiness remains unsupported, autonomous commercial game design remains unsupported, broad production audio quality remains unsupported, and general production renderer quality remains unsupported.

backendReadiness.graphics: NullRenderer fallback plus host-gated D3D12 package window smoke, native 2D sprite package proof with atlas-backed scene sprite plan counters, selected world/UI/effects sprite batch budget profile counters, renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters, selected dense sprite throughput workload counters, and Vulkan package window smoke through packaged shader artifacts. Sprite atlas source authoring remains a reviewed tooling/source contract only; runtime image decoding, renderer/RHI residency from source images, public native or RHI handle access, broad production sprite batching readiness beyond selected plan/budget/native-execution/throughput evidence, cross-backend parity by inference, Metal readiness by inference, and general production renderer quality remain unsupported.
backendReadiness.audio: device-independent cooked audio payload mixed through mirakana_audio with host-gated audio_production_* review evidence
backendReadiness.ui: mirakana_ui-headless renderer submission plus Runtime UI Workbench package evidence through --require-runtime-ui-workbench, Runtime UI Production Stack selected package evidence through --require-runtime-ui-production-stack, selected Windows TSF native IME session evidence through --require-runtime-ui-tsf-session, selected Windows UIA accessibility publication evidence through --require-runtime-ui-uia-publication, cooked UI atlas renderer handoff and UiRendererSubmission execution counters through --require-runtime-ui-renderer-atlas-handoff, and first-party runtime UI platform package proof through --require-runtime-ui-platform-package without external engine parity claims or public native handles
backendReadiness.physics: MK_physics 2D deterministic contact and trigger proof through --require-gameplay-systems
backendReadiness.navigation: MK_navigation grid path planning and agent update proof through --require-gameplay-systems
backendReadiness.ai: MK_ai behavior authoring, perception-blackboard, and behavior-tree proof plus MK_runtime quest/dialogue progression, inventory items crafting, RPG systems review, construction placement validation, procedural generation, entity scale/culling package evidence, scripting sandbox policy package evidence, networking foundation policy package evidence, network production security threat-model/package evidence, simulation orchestration package evidence, MK_tools gameplay authoring review, sandbox-world authoring review, and production authoring workflow package evidence, and MK_runtime_scene placement/procedural-placement intent proof through --require-gameplay-systems, --require-procedural-generation, --require-entity-scale-culling, --require-scripting-sandbox-policy, --require-networking-foundation-policy, --require-simulation-orchestration, --require-gameplay-authoring-review, --require-sandbox-authoring-review, and --require-production-authoring-workflows

budgetRows.notes: Selected performance baseline smoke requires deterministic frame samples before this row can prove the selected package lane.
Selected performance baseline smoke reports p99 in microseconds for the same fixed frame sample set as p95.
Selected sprite batch budget smoke keeps draw rows bounded for the generated 2D package.
Selected sandbox package budget smoke reports deterministic package byte limits without package mutation.
Residency budget is descriptor and smoke evidence only; it is not allocator or GPU budget enforcement.
Selected long-run readiness smoke must report zero over-budget frames for the deterministic short soak before any longer host-owned soak is promoted.
Selected long-run readiness smoke must report zero memory growth with positive high-water evidence; this is not allocator enforcement or broad memory optimization.
Selected dense arena workload is capped at the package-visible 4096 visible sprite row and does not claim backend renderer quality.
Selected projectile storm workload records logical sprite pressure before culling; it is not a broad bullet-hell optimization claim.
Selected sprite throughput smoke bounds package-visible upload bytes without taking renderer/RHI residency ownership.
Selected sandbox tilemap workload uses the existing package tile chunk renderer proof.
Selected sandbox tilemap workload keeps draw rows deterministic and renderer-neutral.
Selected sandbox package budget smoke records safe-point streaming intent without broad background streaming.
Selected UI overlay workload uses cooked UI atlas renderer handoff rows only.
Selected UI overlay workload records atlas budget rows without live glyph atlas generation or renderer upload ownership.
Selected UI overlay workload records one deterministic renderer execution layer.
Selected UI overlay workload records deterministic batch grouping over cooked atlas resources.
Selected UI overlay workload records clip and scissor evidence without renderer-native handles.
Selected UI overlay workload requires cooked glyph and image atlas residency to resolve before submit.
Selected UI overlay workload forbids public native or RHI handle exposure.
Hot reload productization stays host-gated in this workload matrix until external recook-to-resident replacement evidence is imported.
The workload matrix rejects package-script execution, arbitrary shell, and in-process hot reload claims.
Selected short long-run readiness is fixed at the deterministic package smoke sample count; the 108000-frame soak remains opt-in host evidence.

evidenceRows.source: Package-visible performance_baseline_* counters from installed 2D performance baseline smoke: performance_baseline_counter_name=sample_2d.frame_time_us, performance_baseline_profile_name=sample_2d.frame, performance_baseline_frame_p95_us, performance_baseline_frame_p99_us, performance_baseline_frame_budget_us, and zero diagnostics/over-budget counters.

Package-visible long_run_readiness_* counters from installed 2D long-run readiness smoke: long_run_readiness_status=ready, ready=1, frames=3, warmup_frames=0, p95/p99/max frame time 16000us, over_budget_frames=0, positive memory_high_water_bytes, memory_growth_bytes=0, diagnostics=0, shutdown_clean=1, and zero Linux affinity, NUMA, broad SIMD, GPU async overlap, CUDA, HIP, SYCL, and native-handle side effects.
sprite_batch_budget_* counters from installed package smoke
sandbox_package_budget_* counters from installed package smoke

Host-attached Chrome Trace Event JSON artifact for the selected D3D12 package lane. The artifact must include sample_2d.frame duration events or equivalent first-party frame/profile rows before it can upgrade this host-gated row.

Package-visible 2d_sprite_throughput_* counters from installed 2D sprite throughput smoke, including dense arena, projectile storm, upload bytes, retained timing artifact hash, clean diagnostics, and zero broad optimization/backend/Metal claim counters.

Package-visible sandbox_package_budget_*, tilemap_*, and tile_chunk_renderer_* counters from selected sandbox/tilemap package smoke with clean diagnostics and zero renderer/RHI ownership side effects.

Package-visible runtime_ui_renderer_atlas_handoff_* and ui_renderer_* counters from cooked UI atlas handoff smoke with deterministic execution rows, zero native handle, zero source decode, zero live glyph generation, and zero renderer upload side effects.

External operator playtest evidence under games/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package/reports/playtest/latest may later attach reviewed recook-to-resident replacement rows. This matrix keeps the row host-gated and non-executing.

Package-visible long_run_readiness_* counters from selected deterministic short long-run smoke, including three frames, zero over-budget frames, positive memory high-water, zero memory growth, and zero native/SIMD/GPU async/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side effects.
Optional host-2d-long-run-readiness-soak evidence for a 108000-frame package soak. It is not part of default validation and must not promote broad long-running or broad optimization readiness.
